what_it_does: Declares a BINDING dict 'grouped' that renders activity with sectors being a handful of kind-FAMILIES rather than raw kinds: a 'groups' map assigns glob patterns of kind-ids (e.g. corpus.*, chat/voice, create/move/connect) to seven families (memory, conversation, making, operations, signals, decisions, field). angle_from='kind-group', radius_from='time', order_by='declared'. Also declares GROUP_META giving operator-facing name/is copy for each of the seven families.
observations: BINDING id 'grouped', label 'Activity — grouped into families'.
Header comment frames this as 'ONE LENS' demoted from 'the instrument' to one binding among many per Tim's correction.
groups maps families to glob lists; 'field' uses ['*'] as the catch-all 'Everything else'.
order_by='declared' preserves the declared family order.
GROUP_META supplies {name, is} for all seven families, kept beside the groups so a new family + its meaning land together (no drift).
Comment notes projection.py reads GROUP_META for the 'kind-group' sector domain, which was previously unmapped (humanize-only).
Glob sets are described as grounding the meaning (not invented); copy is TENTATIVE.
standouts: Self-documented demotion: explicitly recast from 'the instrument' to one binding among many.
Co-location discipline: GROUP_META kept beside groups specifically to prevent meaning/definition drift.
conventions: Follows the BINDING-dict pattern but uses a 'meta' block for the view AND a separate GROUP_META for the sectors.
Glob-pattern grouping (corpus.*, voice.*, run.*) is the binding-specific mechanism.
Cross-references sibling legibility shape ({id:{name,is}}) used by kinds/raw.py and nodes/_meta.py.
Catch-all 'field' family using ['*'] is a deliberate fallback convention.
notes: The grouped/families binding: collapses many activity kinds into seven globbed families with co-located human meaning, explicitly one lens not the default.
questions: How are glob conflicts resolved when a kind matches both a specific family and the 'field' ['*'] catch-all (precedence by declared order)?
Who maintains the glob sets as new kinds are added so families stay accurate?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — comment promises a coarser families view of activity, and the groups+GROUP_META encode exactly seven globbed families with operator copy.
apparent_themes: Coarser/calmer grain as a deliberate choice, not the default
Co-location to prevent drift between definition and meaning
Separation of machine kind-ids from operator language
intention_signals: Trying to give the operator a calmer, easier-to-read view by gathering many event kinds into a few intuitive families, while explicitly signaling this is a choice among many bindings and keeping each family's meaning next to its definition.
novelty: The explicit demotion narrative plus the discipline of binding glob definitions and human copy together in one file to prevent drift.
contribution: Contributes the grouped/families binding: the seven-family glob map, the catch-all fallback, and the co-located GROUP_META operator copy for the kind-group sector domain.
summary_for_embedding: A binding-config Python file declaring the 'grouped' activity view that maps event-kind globs into seven families (memory, conversation, making, operations, signals, decisions, field) with co-located GROUP_META human-meaning copy.
intention_for_embedding: To let an operator read activity at a calmer, coarser grain by gathering many kinds into a handful of named families.
